Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The paper measures and discusses brassinosteroids (BRs), and CPD is explicitly described as a key BR biosynthesis gene.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Castasterone (CS) is explicitly measured as a late BR product/intermediate increased in ces-D, and CPD is placed in BR biosynthesis connected to late BR formation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: BR measurements were performed on 'aerial plant parts' of 30-day/4-week-old plants.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana is explicitly stated throughout the paper.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana is the applied species in assays.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Col-0 is the wild-type background used for analyses.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: CPD is explicitly discussed as one of the BR-biosynthetic genes.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The paper states CPD is a key BR biosynthesis gene and shows it in the pathway context, but does not explicitly label CPD as belonging to a 'late brassinosteroid biosynthetic pathway' branch.

Enzyme_Name.reason: CPD is the standard gene symbol for Constitutive Photomorphogenesis and Dwarfism; this naming is directly reflected by the paper's use of CPD and prior literature context.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: CPD is described as a cytochrome P450 in the broader literature, but the exact designation 'cytochrome P450 monooxygenase (CYP90A1)' does not appear in the supplied paper text.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper states CES expression pattern overlaps with key BR biosynthesis genes including CPD and ROT3, especially in vascular tissues and early developmental stages.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Results show CPD is elevated in ces-D, Brz2001 induction is reduced in ces-1, and CPD is slightly reduced in 35Sp:c-Myc-CES-SRDX/203.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: 24-epiBL and Brz2001 treatments were explicitly applied in expression/hypocotyl assays involving CPD.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper treats CPD as a BR-biosynthetic cytochrome P450 target but does not explicitly describe the exact oxidation reaction catalyzed by CPD in the supplied text.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: The specific wording 'mid-to-late pathway' is not stated for CPD.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: CPD is presented as a BR-biosynthetic gene contributing to production of late BR intermediates including CS, so precursor/intermediate connection to castasterone is directly inferable.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper explicitly refers to CPD among 'key BR biosynthesis genes'; it does not claim rate-limiting status, matching the field wording.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: In ces-D, higher CPD expression accompanies increased late BR intermediates and BR-related phenotypes; reduced CPD expression in CES repression backgrounds correlates with BR-deficient phenotypes.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summarized points are supported: CPD transcripts elevated in ces-D; Brz2001-mediated CPD induction reduced in ces-1; CES binds CPD promoter by ChIP and G-box by EMSA.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These methods are all reported in relation to CPD: qRT-PCR/semi-quantitative RT-PCR, ChIP-qPCR, EMSA, and BR profiling in CES genotypes.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a forward-looking interpretation; the paper does not directly discuss CPD as an engineering target.

Other_Important_Info.reason: CPD is indeed a direct CES target repeatedly validated in Results, while CES is the main subject of the paper.

Omics_Data.reason: The paper explicitly reports whole-genome Arabidopsis Affymetrix Gene Chip analysis; no accession is given, so 'accession NA' is appropriate.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper repeatedly links BRs to regulation of plant growth and development.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: CES binds the CPD promoter; CPD is not a protein shown to physically interact with CES. If intended as regulatory interactor, this is only indirect, not a protein-protein interaction.

Applied_Promoters.reason: CPD was analysed in its endogenous/native promoter context; no transgenic CPD promoter construct was claimed in this field.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: CES is characterized as a positive regulator of brassinosteroid biosynthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Castasterone (CS) is explicitly measured and increased in ces-D; CES is linked to this late BR product.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: BR levels were measured in aerial parts of adult plants.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Col-0 is the wild-type background used.

Applied_Species.reason: All experimental manipulations are in Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana is the applied species.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Col-0 is the main experimental background.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: CES is identified as locus At1g25330.

Regulator_Type.reason: CES is explicitly identified as a bHLH transcription factor.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper states CES contains a bHLH domain, was named bHLH075, and assigned to subfamily 18.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: CES-YFP localizes diffusely in the nucleus and relocalizes to speckled/subnuclear compartments after BR or Bikinin treatment.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper details CESpro:GUS expression in all organs, enriched in young tissues/vascular bundles, roots, hypocotyls, cotyledons, leaf vasculature, axillary meristems, and stigma, with predominance in vascular tissues during early development.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: CES is overexpressed in ces-D and 2x35Sp:CES lines, full-length transcript is lost in ces-1, and 35Sp:CES-YFP is expressed in protoplasts/plants.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: These conditions/treatments are all explicitly used in CES-related assays: 24-epiBL, Brz2001, Bikinin, light-grown and dark-grown seedlings.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: CES localization responds to BR and Bikinin, and CES is shown as an in vitro substrate of BIN2.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: Direct targets shown are CPD and CYP718 by ChIP; CES also positively affects DWF4, CPD, and ROT3 expression.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: Results support positive regulation of DWF4, CPD, and ROT3 and direct promoter binding to CPD and CYP718.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: CES is presented as regulating BR biosynthesis and affecting BR-responsive transcription.

Regulation_Mode.reason: CES acts via transcriptional regulation, G-box binding, and is modulated by BIN2-mediated phosphorylation in vitro.

Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation.reason: The paper explicitly frames CES as part of BR homeostasis/feedback adjustment of BR-biosynthetic gene expression.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: CES homodimerizes and interacts with BEE1 and BEE3 in yeast; CES-BEE1 interaction is shown in planta.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: BEE1 and BEE3 interactions are shown in yeast, BEE1 in planta, and BIN2 phosphorylates CES in vitro.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Overexpression/activation increases late BR accumulation and BR-response phenotypes; loss/repression causes BR-deficient phenotypes.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: CES is shown as a positive regulator required for proper BR-biosynthetic gene expression and BR homeostasis, particularly early in development.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are explicitly used for CES in the Results.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each numbered finding is directly supported by the Results and figures.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper does not directly discuss applied breeding/engineering use; this is a reasonable extrapolation but not explicitly supported.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper states CES transcript abundance is not BL regulated, unlike BEE1; redundancy masking knockout phenotypes is also discussed. 'DOI not present' is true for supplied text but not a scientific claim from Results.

Omics_Data.reason: Whole-genome Arabidopsis Affymetrix Gene Chip analysis is explicitly reported; no accession is provided.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper consistently ties BRs to control of growth, development, hypocotyl elongation, leaf morphology, and meristem activity.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: These phenotypes are explicitly described for CES overexpression/ces-D and CES-SRDX plants.

Applied_Promoters.reason: 2x35S and CaMV 35S promoters are explicitly used for CES constructs.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: BEE1 is linked to BR responses and CES mechanism, but this paper does not directly test BEE1 against a specific terminal BR metabolite class/output such as metabolite accumulation.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The paper does not directly connect BEE1 experimentally to castasterone accumulation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: No BEE1-specific metabolite accumulation measurements were performed in aerial parts or elsewhere.

Source_Species.reason: BEE1 constructs and assays are in Arabidopsis.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana is the species used in protoplast/in planta assays.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The work is generally in Col-0 background, but BEE1-specific experiments are mostly transient assays and the field is not explicitly stated for BEE1.

Applied_Species.reason: BEE1 is tested in Arabidopsis systems.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana is the applied species.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: While the paper broadly uses Col-0, BEE1 transient assays do not explicitly restate the variety for this gene-specific context.

Regulator_Type.reason: BEE1 is described as a close homologue of CES and a bHLH protein/BR-response regulator from prior work.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: BEE1 is explicitly referred to as a bHLH transcription factor/homologue.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: BEE1-YFP shows diffuse nuclear localization and relocalizes to distinct nuclear compartments after BL or Bikinin treatment.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper explicitly states BEE1 expression is BR/BL inducible based on prior work.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: BEE1 was tested as a YFP fusion in protoplasts.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: 24-epiBL and Bikinin are explicitly used in BEE1 localization experiments.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: BR/BL is explicitly an input affecting BEE1 behavior in this study and prior literature context.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: This paper does not directly test BEE1 target genes; only a proposed role with CES is discussed.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: BEE1 is linked to BR-responsive transcription and proposed CES-BEE1 regulation, but no direct BEE1 control of BR biosynthesis is experimentally shown here.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Protein interaction/heterodimeric transcriptional regulation with CES is directly supported by Y2H and BiFC evidence.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: BEE1 interacts with CES in yeast and in planta and colocalizes with CES in BR-induced nuclear compartments.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: CES is directly shown to interact with BEE1.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: This study does not test BEE1 mutant/overexpression phenotypes; any effect is inferred from interaction with CES and prior work.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: No direct evidence in this paper shows BEE1 has a decisive influence on castasterone or another terminal BR product.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Y2H, BiFC, and co-localization in protoplasts are all explicitly used for BEE1.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are supported: prior BR-response role cited, colocalization with CES shown here, and CES-BEE1 interaction shown in yeast and in planta.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper does not discuss BEE1 as an engineering target; this is speculative.

Other_Important_Info.reason: BEE1 is not the primary subject for metabolite changes, but it is experimentally validated as part of CES mechanism.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper generally states BRs regulate plant growth and development.

Applied_Promoters.reason: BEE1-YFP was generated under control of the CaMV 35S promoter.
